findings.description: The primary>secondary>tertiary palette hierarchy/application ratio (blues/golds dominant, tertiary as accent) is a distinct governing rule with no corresponding candidate rule at all — entire unit is uncaptured.

payload.description: Apply palette in primary>secondary>tertiary ratio: blues/golds dominate, tertiary colors are accents/surfaces.

findings.description: Headline and body text color assignments (Brand Blue/White for headlines; Graphite/White for body) are distinct color_application rules never clustered into any candidate rule.

payload.description: Headlines: Brand Blue (White on dark). Body text: Graphite #212121 on light, White on dark.

findings.description: Chart color rules including the strict reservation of Sunshine/Gold for key figures are entirely uncaptured; this is a hard restriction ("strictly reserved") that should likely be hard_constraint, not just missing.

payload.description: Charts: Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for key figures only; gray for placebo.

findings.description: Coral usage restriction (never for primary CTAs/headlines/data; sparing tertiary accent only) plus the [GENERAL] email-avoidance clause is completely absent from candidate rules — a distinct must_not rule with an embedded governance-scoped sub-rule.

payload.description: Coral is a sparing tertiary accent only; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL]: avoid Coral in email sections unless brief calls for it.

findings.description: Source explicitly tags this rule '[GENERAL]', indicating a governance scope/applicability marker (general-audience guidance), but the candidate rule carries no governance facet reflecting this scope designation.

Source unit states tint percentages descriptively without any explicit normative modal ('must'/'should'); asserting polarity=must and hardness=strong_default may overstate strength versus e.g. headline/body rules of similar descriptive phrasing — worth verifying against sibling rules for consistent hardness calibration.
Confirmed correct: hard_constraint/must aligns with explicit 'must be maintained in all instances' language; no error, included for completeness of governance cross-check.
